title: lappula caspia
intro: Lappula caspia, also known as the Kazakh bugloss, is a plant species in the borage family (Boraginaceae) native to the steppes of Central Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Lappula caspia include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and improving respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of Lappula caspia can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to support the immune system and alleviate digestive issues.
